Patch Set 5:

James, why not get rid of all of the instances of cfs_size*
Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 5:

Removing cfs_size_* from libcfs and the kernel modules is large work and at this stage for 2.11 I didn't want to block any important patches from landing. This patch is much lighter so it should block very few things.

Patch Set 5:

Ben okay with my explanation?

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Yep, makes sense
Patch Set 5: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as e83f6e4fc7c3e2681bee744563d80cd45db95a4d by Oleg Drokin

commit.subject: LU-6142 UAPI: replace cfs_size_* macros with __ALIGN_KERNEL
commit.message:
LU-6142 UAPI: replace cfs_size_* macros with __ALIGN_KERNEL

The lustre specific cfs_size_* macros can be easily replaced with
the __ALIGN_KERNEL macro provided by the linux kernel for our
user land code. This brings us closer to building against the
upstream client.

Change-Id: I5cd261807f60296eaac884b66f084c128adc5b01
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30379
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Ben Evans <bevans@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
